A script to check for and report trailing spaces in source files.

# Purpose
This script is written in the Tcl programming language and is intended to be executed using the Tcl shell (`tclsh`). The primary function of the script is to check for trailing spaces in the lines of source files. It iterates over a collection of source files obtained from the `getSourceFileNames` function. For each file, it reads all lines using the `getAllLines` function.

The script processes each line of the source files to identify trailing spaces using a regular expression. If a trailing space is detected, the script calls the `report` function to log the occurrence, specifying the file name, line number, and a message indicating that trailing spaces are not allowed. This functionality is useful for maintaining code quality by ensuring that source files do not contain unnecessary whitespace at the end of lines.
